findings: Passed visual anatomy check: the principal bird has one head, one broad black bill, two legs, two webbed feet, two wings (one visible, one occluded), and one tail; eye, bill profile, neck, breast, wing feathering, legs, and feet are consistent with Canada Goose.

The black head and long neck, white cheek patches joined below the chin, tan-gray breast, brown back/wings, black legs/feet, and dark tail with contrasting pale rump/undertail treatment agree with the field-reference photos and verified descriptions.

All visible text is correct: Canada Goose; Branta canadensis; Anatidae; length 76–110 cm (29.9–43.3 in); wingspan 127–170 cm (50.0–66.9 in); and weight 3.0–9.0 kg (105.8–317.5 oz). Habitat, behavior, and field-mark text are supported by the verified guide pages.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.
No concrete material species, anatomy, or text issues found.
